Container.Name: Kaval
Container.Overview:
Kaval is a self-hosted observability and investigation platform for Unraid homelabs. This template is intentionally scoped for private and self-hosted testing before any public Community Apps submission.
It installs one Docker container with two internal processes. kaval-core serves the UI and API on port 9800. kaval-executor handles approval-gated restart actions over an internal Unix socket while remaining inside the same container runtime approved by CR-0002 and ADR-014.
Build or publish the private-testing image tag from this repository first, then use this template to deploy on Unraid and complete guided setup in the web UI.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: HTTP port for the Kaval UI and API.

Config.Name: AppData
Config.Description: Persistent Kaval state, including kaval.db and kaval.yaml.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Required so the internal executor process can perform approval-gated container restart actions.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Optional container timezone for logs and scheduled quiet-hours defaults.

Config.Name: Approval HMAC Secret
Config.Description: Required. Long random secret used to sign and verify approval tokens between the core and executor processes.

Config.Name: Widget Public URL
Config.Description: Optional externally reachable base URL used when Kaval generates widget links.

Config.Name: Local Model Name
Config.Description: Optional bootstrap model name for an OpenAI-compatible local model endpoint.

Config.Name: Local Model Base URL
Config.Description: Optional bootstrap base URL for the local model endpoint.

Config.Name: Notification URLs
Config.Description: Optional comma-separated Apprise destinations for bootstrap notification routing.
